name: Ward the Standing Stones
description: Something has been disturbing the old wards at the forest boundary. A small group can drive it back if they arrive rested and fed. It should not take long.
flavorLine: Routine work. They know the ground. Should be back by nightfall.

name: Drive Back the Blight
description: A section of the old growth has gone dark. The work is slow and demands absolute clarity of purpose — the blight responds to hesitation. They will need to be sharp for this.
flavorLine: The blight is patient. A poorly-provisioned group will not be.

name: Walk the Old Deep Roads
description: The deep roads beneath the forest have not been traveled in a generation. What has settled there in the meantime is unknown. They will need to be able to go the full distance.
flavorLine: No one knows what lives down there now. Send them ready, or do not send them.

name: Clear the Low Tunnels
description: The lower access tunnels have been blocked again. Cave-ins or something worse — hard to tell until you're in it. A straightforward job for a well-fed crew.
flavorLine: Tight, dark, and damp. Nothing they haven't handled before.

name: Hold the Mountain Pass
description: Somebody needs to hold a strategic pass through the range against a group who wants it more than they should. The cold at that elevation is significant. Warm food before they go.
flavorLine: The cold at that elevation is not a small thing. A poorly-fed company will not hold.

name: Descend to the Deep Forge
description: The deep forge has not been lit in living memory. Getting it operational again means going further down than the company has gone before. The temperature at those depths is not survivable without preparation.
flavorLine: No one has been that deep in living memory. There are reasons for that.

name: Outrun the Harbor Watch
description: A simple extraction job. Get in, get the cargo, get out before the harbor watch finishes their circuit. Easy money if everyone is fast.
flavorLine: Quick in, quick out. Easy money if they keep their heads.

name: Raid the Merchant Convoy
description: A merchant convoy with better-than-expected security. The take is worth it, but the window is narrow and the margin for error is smaller than they would like. Speed is the only answer.
flavorLine: Better security than expected. The margin is tight. Mistakes will cost.

name: Break the Siege at Ironport
description: The port has been blockaded for three weeks. Speed gets them through the harbor mouth. What follows is a grinding fight that goes through the night and into the next day — the kind that breaks people who haven't eaten right.
flavorLine: A grinding fight through the night. Not everyone walks away from those.

name: Track the Border Crossing
description: Something has been crossing the old boundary markers at night. Not large. Not obvious. Exactly the kind of thing that becomes a serious problem if left alone. A careful eye is what this requires, not force.
flavorLine: A careful job. They know this ground. Should be manageable.

name: Read the Waystone Marks
description: The old waystones along the northern track have been marked by someone who knows the old language. Reading them correctly means understanding what is coming and from where. Getting it wrong means being in the wrong place entirely.
flavorLine: Old language, old dangers. This is not something you walk into unprepared.

name: Find the Lost Garrison
description: A garrison of twelve went into the deep borderlands two months ago and has not been heard from since. Finding them — or what remains — means reading a landscape that has been deliberately obscured.
flavorLine: Twelve went in two months ago. None returned. Send them ready, or do not send them at all.
